topic: A student discovers their teacher follows them on social media
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up on my phone screen, a small, unwelcome flicker in the otherwise mundane stream of class updates. It was a follow request. From Mr. Harrison. My stomach did a slow, unpleasant flip. I hadn't interacted with him online at all, not even a like on a shared academic post. I stared at the profile picture—the same slightly too-bright smile he used in lecture—and my fingers hovered over the screen. Why would he want to be connected? I quickly swiped it away, a prickling sensation running down my arms. I glanced over at him in class, watching his movements, trying to read some hidden meaning in the way he adjusted his tie. It felt like I was being watched, cataloged.

[story 2]

I found myself checking my feed every few minutes, a compulsive habit that felt increasingly out of control. It started innocently enough, just seeing a mention of an upcoming event, but now it was focused entirely on Mr. Harrison. His recent posts were curated, professional, yet there was an undeniable intimacy to them—photos of weekend hikes, casual meals, things that felt far removed from the stern, impeccably dressed man who lectured us on historical timelines. When he tagged a location near the library, a cold knot tightened in my chest. I forced a bright, agreeable smile when he spoke to me in the hallway, my voice sounding unnaturally high. I kept my gaze fixed on the floor, acutely aware of every glance he might cast my way.

[story 3]

It was the sheer volume of his activity that started to bother me. He posted pictures of his garden, intricate coffee art, and even a short video of him playing a musical instrument. It was all so personal, so unguarded, and it made the classroom feel suddenly too small, too confined. I started avoiding eye contact during discussions, finding myself fiddling incessantly with the edge of my notebook. When he asked a question, my response was clipped, almost too quick. I noticed the way his eyes lingered on me for a fraction of a second too long when I answered, and a wave of unease washed over me, making my palms instantly damp. I needed to figure out what the pattern was, what the underlying connection was.
